<context>This chunk defines key concepts related to Investment-Linked Policies (ILPs), including pricing mechanisms like forward pricing, and details various fees and charges such as sub-fund management fees, benefit/insurance charges, policy fees, administrative charges, surrender charges, and the investment strategy of dollar cost averaging.</context>
investment sub-funds shall be determined by reference to the offerprice established on the next valuation date. In essence, it means that the next available offer price after the premium has been paid.

- Definition or Explanation: Sub-Fund ManagementFee
A sub-fund management fee is paid to the fund manager for portfolio supervisionand for general management of the sub-fund affairs. Such fee also serves tocompensate the manager for the expenses incurred in providing the relevant services. There will normally be a sub-fund management charge likely to range from0.5% to 2% per annum. The level of this charge will depend on the competition, the type of assets under management, and the profit requirements of the life insurers.

- Definition or Explanation: Benefit / InsuranceCharges
These are charges incurred by the client for the insurer to provide coverage forcertain events like death, total and permanent disability or critical illness, etc.occurring during the policy period of insurance for the ILP.

- Definition or Explanation: Policy Fees
These cover the administrative expenses of setting up the policy, as well as theregular running expenses of administering the policy.

- Definition or Explanation: Administrative Charges
These cover the initial expenses of the policy.The cost of administration is incurredlargely to provide record-keeping and transaction services.

- Definition or Explanation: Surrender Charges
Surrender charges or back-end loads are the charges that a client would incur should he decide to cash out a portion or allof his units before a certain period oftime. This serves to compensate the insurer for the costs incurred in the setup andthe administration of the policy. Surrender charges are usually a percentage of thetotal surrender value that a client willbe cashing out or surrendering. The chargesusually decrease from the first year onwards.

- Definition or Explanation: Dollar Cost Averaging
Dollar cost averaging simply means investing the same dollar amount in a sub- fund at regular intervals, regardless of the sub-fund price fluctuations. By regularlyinvesting specified amounts of money over a period of time, the policy owner is ableto capitalise on market cycles, by purchasing more units when prices are low andfewer units when prices are high.

table narrative: ["For Investment-Linked Policies (ILPs), ILP sub-funds are subject to the requirements as specified in the Insurance Regulations - Insurance Act 1966 and Notice No: MAS307.", "For Unit Trusts (UTs), investments are subject to requirements as specified in the Code on Collective Investment Schemes and regulated under the Securities and Futures Act 2001.", "At the point of sale, Investment-Linked Policies (ILPs) require a Copy Of The CoverPage, Product Summary, Policy Illustration, Bundled Product Disclosure Document, and Guide to Life Insurance (from LIA).", "At the point of sale, Unit Trusts (UTs) require a Prospectus / Profile Statement.", "Post-sale, Investment-Linked Policies (ILPs) provide a Policy contract, Annual Statement of Accounts, and Annual and Semi-Annual Report on ILP sub-fund.", "Post-sale, Unit Trusts (UTs) provide an Annual Statement of Accounts and Annual and Semi-Annual Report.", "For Investment-Linked Policies (ILPs), the appropriate level of death benefits for ILPs offered in Singapore is left to market forces.", "For Unit Trusts (UTs), death benefits are not applicable.", "The free-look or cancellation period for Investment-Linked Policies (ILPs) is 14 days from the date of receipt of policy, and the insurer is entitled to make adjustments to reflect changes in market values of underlying assets.", "The free-look or cancellation period for Unit Trusts (UTs) is seven calendar days from the date of signing of the Purchase Agreement, and the relevant person is entitled to an adjustment to reflect the change in market values of the units held by the investor.", "For Investment-Linked Policies (ILPs), the treatment of rounding differences and compensation for computational errors refers to Notice No: MAS 307
